headline: Penang Bridge Toll Revenue Funds Mainland — Not Penang Development
context: Civil society groups revealed that RM4.8 billion in cumulative Penang Bridge toll revenue collected since 1985 was channelled to federal consolidated revenue rather than ringfenced for Penang infrastructure, despite the bridge being built to serve the state.

cards.big: RM4.8 billion collected from Penang Bridge tolls since 1985 — zero reinvested in Penang's crumbling infrastructure
cards.sub: Penang contributes 6.5% of national GDP but receives only 2.8% of federal development allocation

cards.big: Penang's federal allocation per capita at RM920 ranks 11th of 13 states despite being 3rd in GDP contribution
cards.sub: State government self-finances 58% of development from own sources — highest self-dependency ratio in Malaysia

cards.big: Penang's public transport coverage at 28% of residential areas — worst among states with population above 1 million
cards.sub: Daily traffic congestion costs Penang economy RM2.4 billion annually in lost productivity and fuel waste

cards.big: Toll concession agreements under OSA prevent public scrutiny of revenue sharing between federal and state governments
cards.sub: No mechanism for state governments to audit or challenge federal allocation of toll revenues collected within their borders

cards.big: Collecting billions from Penang Bridge users while underinvesting in Penang's infrastructure is fiscal extraction dressed up as national development
Should bridge and highway toll revenue be constitutionally earmarked for infrastructure development in the state where it's collected?
